issues_found: Figure 5 (diagnostic heatmap) shows half_lambda BEM = 0.000 while Table 6 reports BEM = 0.500. Same class of figure-table contradiction bug that plagued Figures 3/4 in prior iterations -- fixed in scatter plot (Figure 4) but not propagated to heatmap.

Figure 8 (theorem2_validation.png) includes PMP-WD data points that are not in the paper's method set (Section 4.1 lists 7 methods; PMP-WD is not among them). The figure references a nonexistent 'Theorem 2'. N=18 data points implies 6 methods x 3 seeds, inconsistent with the 7-method design. Desk-reject-level: a figure showing data for an undescribed method.

No ImageNet experiments despite project constraints mandating them. Flagged for 6+ iterations with zero progress. No root cause diagnosis has been performed. The paper claims 'sufficiently overparameterized problems' but evidence is confined to CIFAR-10/100 (32x32, <=15M params). This is the single largest credibility gap.

Statistical power insufficient for null-result claims. N=3 gives ~15-20% power for 0.5% effects. TOST equivalence confirmed in only 6/12 comparisons at delta=1.0%. Paper headline 'all methods equivalent' is not supported -- 'failed to reject at low power' != 'confirmed equivalence'.

CSI listed as a primary contribution (Section 1.3, item 2) despite zero predictive value (rho<0.3, p>0.3) and arbitrary component weights (0.4, 0.3, 0.3). Sensitivity analysis tests only 3 nearly-identical weight sets. Flagged for 5+ iterations without demotion.

Section 5.7 draws directional conclusions from two non-significant correlations (rho=-0.379, p=0.121; rho=0.045, p=0.858). The claim 'cumulative alignment may be more informative' is statistically improper without a formal Steiger's z-test. Flagged since iter_007.

Abstract implies full factorial design ('7 methods, 2 optimizers, 2 architectures, 2 datasets') suggesting 168 runs. Actual design: 84 (ResNet-20 full matrix) + 21 (VGG-16-BN, SGD/CIFAR-10 only) = 105. Misleading to reviewers who expect VGG-16-BN + AdamW or VGG-16-BN + CIFAR-100.

Title uses 'Why' (causal claim) but paper only demonstrates correlation/observation, not causal mechanism. No ablation of AdamW's second-moment, no causal intervention experiment, no theoretical derivation of why adaptive scaling subsumes Phi modulation.

NoBN ablation data from iter_005 exists (constant: 87.74+/-0.21, CWD: 87.62+/-0.13, spread 0.12pp) but only 2/7 methods tested. Section 6.2 reports these preliminary results, but 0.12pp spread WITHOUT BN is narrower than 0.25pp WITH BN under AdamW -- contradicting the BN-mechanism hypothesis. Paper hand-waves with 'limiting generalizability' rather than addressing the contradiction.

Proposal.md still references 4 theorems and 2 propositions (Lyapunov, PMP, controller taxonomy, formal budget equivalence theorem) that are not in the delivered paper. Future planners/critics compare paper against proposal and penalize the gap. Figure filename 'theorem2_validation.png' is vestigial.
issues_fixed: Figure 3(a) PMP-WD contamination: Figure 3 now shows correct 7 methods with 'Spread: 0.25pp' matching Table 2 and Section 5.1 text. No PMP-WD in Figure 3. (iter_009 S1/S3/S5: FIXED)
Figure 4 (BEM scatter plot) half_lambda position: Now correctly shows half_lambda at BEM ~0.5, matching Table 6. (iter_009 S2: FIXED)
Data provenance mismatch between Figure 3 and Table 2: Both now use iter_003 data. Cross-verified against 42 raw summary.json files. (iter_009 S1: FIXED)
Triple spread contradiction (0.49pp vs 0.25pp vs 0.25pp): All three sources (Figure 3, Section 5.1, Table 2) now consistently show 0.25pp. (iter_009 S3: FIXED)
Certified Band / Lyapunov V_t contradiction section: Removed in iter_009. (iter_008 S2: FIXED)
